Sarcasm is a form of verbal irony where someone says the opposite of what they actually mean, usually to mock or express contempt. So the key here is that the speaker is saying one thing but actually meaning the opposite. Looking at option (A): "Stop putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point — good idea." Or maybe the other way around. Option (B): "Keep putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point — good idea."
